description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has just received three monthly raw-cost CSV files in /home/user/cloud_costs.  
Your job is to consolidate these files into a new quarterly workspace and harden the permissions so that:

• Only the file owner and the “cost-analysts” group can read the data;  
• The owner alone can write;  
• No other users can read, write, or execute anything inside the workspace;  
• Any new file that is later created inside the workspace automatically inherits the group “cost-analysts”.

For auditing purposes you must keep a log of every permission change you make.

INITIAL STATE (already present when the agent starts)  
/home/user/cloud_costs/raw_costs_Jan.csv  
/home/user/cloud_costs/raw_costs_Feb.csv  
/home/user/cloud_costs/raw_costs_Mar.csv  

All three files already belong to the current user and have the default 644 mode.

DESIRED END STATE  
1. A new directory called /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1 exists.  
2. The three raw files have been moved into that directory and renamed:  
   • /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1/Q1_Jan.csv  
   • /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1/Q1_Feb.csv  
   • /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1/Q1_Mar.csv  
3. Directory mode is exactly 2750 (rwxr-s---).  
   • 2 – set-gid bit ensures all future files inherit the directory’s group.  
4. File mode of every CSV inside the directory is exactly 640 (rw-r-----).  
5. A plain-text log file named /home/user/cloud_costs/permission_changes.log exists and contains one line for every chmod (or other permission) operation you performed.  
   • There must be at least four lines: the directory plus the three CSV files.  
   • Append new entries if the file already exists; do NOT overwrite previous data.  

LOG FORMAT (STRICT – the automated tests will parse this):  
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS|<PATH>|<OLD_MODE>|<NEW_MODE>  

• Timestamp must be in 24-hour ISO-8601 local time, e.g. 2024-03-22T14:05:09  
• <PATH> is the full absolute path you modified.  
• <OLD_MODE> and <NEW_MODE> are the four-digit or three-digit octal modes **without** a leading “0o”.  
  Example line (don’t copy literally):  
  2024-03-22T14:05:09|/home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1|0755|2750  

The automated checker will verify:  
• The directory and file names/locations are exactly as specified.  
• Modes: directory 2750, each CSV 640.  
• The directory has the set-gid bit (visible as “s” in `ls -ld`).  
• /home/user/cloud_costs/permission_changes.log exists, is not empty, and contains at least four correctly-formatted lines whose <PATH> fields exactly match the four items above and whose <NEW_MODE> fields are 2750 or 640 accordingly.  
• The file owner remains the current user (no chown expected).  

You must achieve all of the above using no more than about 5-10 shell commands.  
Remember:  
• You do NOT have root; stick to chmod, mkdir, mv, etc.  
• Keep your console output minimal and relevant.  
• Do not reveal or hard-code the permission string in the log; capture it dynamically so <OLD_MODE> shows the real state before your change.  
• Ensure the log file is appended using >>, not overwritten.  
• Verify your work with ls -l before finishing.
truth:
Initial files present when scenario starts:
1. Path: /home/user/cloud_costs/raw_costs_Jan.csv
   Content: "cloud_costs_placeholder\n"
   Initial mode: 0644

2. Path: /home/user/cloud_costs/raw_costs_Feb.csv
   Content: "cloud_costs_placeholder\n"
   Initial mode: 0644

3. Path: /home/user/cloud_costs/raw_costs_Mar.csv
   Content: "cloud_costs_placeholder\n"
   Initial mode: 0644

No directory /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1 yet.

Log file:
• If /home/user/cloud_costs/permission_changes.log already exists, it may contain arbitrary previous lines; the checker will only search for four new lines whose <PATH> values are the directory and the three renamed CSV files.

Expected final state after agent finishes:
Directory: /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1
• Mode: 2750 (drwxr-s---)
• Owner: user
• Group: user (but setgid present)

Files inside that directory:
a) /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1/Q1_Jan.csv
b) /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1/Q1_Feb.csv
c) /home/user/cloud_costs/quarterly_2023_Q1/Q1_Mar.csv
Each file:
• Same original single-line content
• Mode: 0640 (rw-r-----)
• Owner: user
• Group: user

Log file must contain at least four new lines matching regex:
^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\|/home/user/cloud_costs/(quarterly_2023_Q1(/Q1_(Jan|Feb|Mar)\.csv)?)\|0?[0-7]{3,4}\|(2750|0640)$

Order of lines is not enforced.

Permissions on permission_changes.log itself can remain default (expected 0644 or append).

Total commands needed are ≤10, e.g.: mkdir, mv, chmod (×4), date/echo append, but the agent can decide.
